metadata.title: Review and where to go next

inlineContent.text: Review what you built and where to find resources to build something you can ship to production.

sections.title: Review and where to go next

inlineContent.text: In this tutorial, you built the server side of a TODO management application in Swift!
Specifically, you:
Started with a simple Swift
service using Vapor that returned a pre-generated response to an
request.
Leveraged
to generate the routes and handlers.
Added in
to interact with a PostgreSQL database.
Integrated
for distributed tracing and metrics, with a Grafana dashboard to visualize them.
This should give you a taste of how to write
services with Swift!
The list below provides a complete list of the tools used:
Swift OpenAPI libraries
to provide the web framework.
for database persistence and migrations.
to add basic logging to the application.
library to coordinate services and signaling.
library to read the application configuration.
for OpenTelemetry integration (tracing and metrics export).
for the metrics API.
for the tracing API.
for automatic CPU, memory, and process metrics.
for local observability visualization.
to help build the swift service and connect to PostgreSQL.

content.text: Where do I go from here?

inlineContent.text: Apart from the tools and libraries we covered in the tutorial, there’s a plethora of options that are available to write Swift services:
HTTP
is the web framework this tutorial used.
is another web framework you could use.
GRPC
is another library you can use to build API focused cloud services in Swift.
OpenAPI provides examples of how to use it as both client and server, with multiple frameworks:

abstract.text: Ensure you have the right tools and example code before learning how to write your Swift server application.
Ensure you have the right tools and example code before learning how to write your Swift server application.

doc://com.apple.ASE.getting-started/tuto.title: Clone and build the tutorial repository

abstract.text: Ensure you have the right tools and example code before learning how to write your Swift server application.

The tutorial example contains a Package.swift file that defines the dependencies and targets for the app, as well as a Sources directory with starter files. The finished tutorial is also provided in the Completed folder.
